sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the green box with white center is now at the top of the left panel (rows around 21-23). Let me check its exact position and see if I can now move RIGHT to go around the purple bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Green objects:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")
        for cid in n['children']:
            c = next(x for x in seg if x['id'] == cid)
            print(f"    child: color={c['color']}, boundary={c['boundary']}")

# Check purple bar boundaries
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        print(f"\nPurple bar: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if green box is above the purple bar
green_top = None
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        green_top = min(r for r, c in n['boundary'])
        break
print(f"\nGreen box top row: {green_top}")
print(f"Purple bar top row: 21")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so the green box is at rows 21-23, cols 12-14. The purple bar starts at row 21, cols 33-38. So the green box is at the same top row as the purple bar. I need to go ABOVE row 21 to get around the bar.
Let me try moving UP one more time to see if I can get above row 21. If the green box moves to rows 18-20, it would be above the purple bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving UP one more time to get above the purple bar
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
